key_provisions: Faculty Guidelines on Using Generative AI Tools issued by Office for Academic Affairs (June 2023)
No assignment substantially generated by AI may be submitted as student's own work
AI-assisted work must be disclosed; no grade may be substantially assigned by AI
StratComm guidelines require explicit disclosure of AI-generated or AI-influenced content
Prohibition on uploading proprietary, confidential, student, personnel, or protected data into AI tools without explicit approval
OIT maintains an approved/restricted/prohibited AI tools list governing enterprise use
ai_policy.summary: UA has not adopted a single unified institution-wide AI policy; instead, guidance is layered across the Office for Academic Affairs (faculty guidelines, June 2023), the Barefield College of Arts & Sciences Statement of Principle, Strategic Communications media guidelines, and an OIT-managed AI Approved List governing enterprise tool use. Core rules are disclosure of AI use, no substitution of AI for student learning or grading, and a prohibition on entering sensitive data into non-approved tools.

governance_structure.notes: No publicly named university-wide AI governance committee at UA Tuscaloosa surfaced in searches (distinct from UAB, which has a formal AI Advisory Committee reporting to the President's Risk Cabinet). AI tool vetting is handled operationally through OIT's AI Approved List process. UA faculty (Matthew Hudnall, Institute of Data & Analytics) served on the state-level Alabama Generative AI Task Force established by Gov. Ivey's EO 738.

vendor_deployments.product: Microsoft Copilot with Enterprise Data Protection (web-based)
vendor_deployments.notes: Free to all students, faculty, and staff with a UA campus email. Prompts/responses encrypted and not used to train Microsoft models. Approved for use without restrictions.

Two-month pilot with 35 staff (content creators, designers, management). Security-approved without restrictions but ROI hard to quantify; $30/user/month cost would be borne by individual departments rather than centrally funded. No campus-wide rollout announced.
Approved; may be used with sensitive and restricted data under OIT Security guidance.
Approved with restrictions - cannot be used with sensitive/restricted data; data improvement features must be disabled. No campus-wide ChatGPT Edu deployment identified.
Approved without restrictions, but cannot be used with HIPAA data.
Approved with restrictions; cannot process sensitive information. No Claude for Education enterprise deployment identified.
Approved without restrictions for transcription, summaries, noise reduction.

ai_academic_programs.name: Bachelor of Science in Artificial Intelligence
Data Science, B.S. (combines machine learning, AI, and ethical data analysis)
AI Essentials (Culverhouse Executive Education graduate-level certificate)

notable_deals_investments.title: High Performance Computing and Data Center
notable_deals_investments.counterparty: U.S. Department of Commerce (Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2023) and State of Alabama
notable_deals_investments.summary: $100M HPC facility announced in 2023 to scale UA's computing capacity roughly 100x, housing 125 server racks with petascale capability. Jointly funded by federal appropriations and the State of Alabama; expected completion 2026. Positioned as foundational infrastructure for statewide AI research.

notable_deals_investments.title: Alabama Center for the Advancement of AI (Styslinger College of Engineering)
notable_deals_investments.summary: Research center within the College of Engineering directed by Dr. Jiaqi (Jackey) Gong, spanning machine learning, computer vision, generative AI, autonomous systems, and robotics.

notable_deals_investments.title: Center for AI Research and Development (Culverhouse College of Business)
notable_deals_investments.summary: Business-school AI research center launched June 2024; Culverhouse also hosts a Group for Research in AI Driven Education.

notable_deals_investments.title: Alabama Higher Education AI Exchange (host institution)
notable_deals_investments.counterparty: Alabama Commission on Higher Education, Oracle, AWS, Google Cloud, Deloitte (sponsors)
notable_deals_investments.summary: UA hosted the inaugural Alabama Higher Education AI Exchange at the Bryant Conference Center on Oct 22, 2025, convening state academic, industry, and government AI leaders. Keynote: Google Public Sector Field CTO Chris Hein.

leadership_statements.speaker: Allen Parrish
leadership_statements.role: Executive Director, Alabama Cyber Institute, University of Alabama
leadership_statements.context: UA News feature 'Positioning UA for an AI Future'
leadership_statements.quote_or_summary: "What we're seeing today in AI is a microcosm of what we'll see in the next five years, and we want to be ready for it."

leadership_statements.speaker: Matthew Hudnall
leadership_statements.role: Assoc. Prof. MIS / Institute of Data & Analytics; UA representative to Alabama GenAI Task Force
leadership_statements.quote_or_summary: "From process automation to quicker access to data, I only see [generative AI] as becoming more integrated into our society."

state_ai_policy_context.summary: Gov. Kay Ivey signed Executive Order 738 on Feb 8, 2024, creating the Alabama Generative AI Task Force (7 cabinet members, 2 higher-ed representatives, 4 legislators). The task force delivered its final report to the Governor in Nov 2024; Ivey publicly released it in March 2025, covering risks, data classification, employee education, procurement guidelines, and an executive-branch AI inventory. Alabama also has a state Generative AI Acceptable Use Policy and an AI Governance Policy published by the state OIT (Jan 2025). UA faculty participated on the task force, but the UA System itself does not appear to have a system-wide AI policy; AI governance is campus-by-campus (UA, UAB, UAH each have their own guidance).

narrative_summary: The University of Alabama is pursuing a pragmatic, infrastructure-first AI posture rather than a splashy enterprise-license play. The big capital bet is a $100M petascale High Performance Computing and Data Center (federal + state funded, completion expected 2026) positioned as statewide research infrastructure. On enterprise deployments, UA has made Microsoft Copilot with Enterprise Data Protection free and campus-wide to all students, faculty, and staff, but the premium Microsoft 365 Copilot remains a small (35-user) staff pilot with no announced rollout due to cost. There is no ChatGPT Edu or Claude for Education campus deployment; OIT instead governs AI through an Approved List that explicitly categorizes ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini as approved-with-restrictions. Governance is notably decentralized: UA has no publicly named university-wide AI committee or Chief AI Officer; policy guidance lives across Academic Affairs (June 2023 faculty guidelines), Strategic Communications, and OIT. Academic capacity is expanding quickly - trustees approved a new B.S. in Artificial Intelligence in Nov 2024, Culverhouse launched a Center for AI R&D in June 2024, and UA hosted the inaugural Alabama Higher Ed AI Exchange in Oct 2025. State-level context is active but low-friction: Gov. Ivey's EO 738 created a GenAI Task Force whose final report (released March 2025) targets state executive-branch agencies, not higher ed mandates. New president Peter J. Mohler (from Ohio State, started July 2025) has not yet made a signature AI pronouncement. For LSU: UA is a structurally similar SEC land-grant peer moving faster on HPC infrastructure and academic programs but slower on enterprise premium AI licensing.
confidence_notes: HIGH confidence on: enterprise vendor deployments (OIT Approved List is authoritative), Copilot pilot details, the $100M HPC investment, the new B.S. in AI, state EO 738, and presidential/CIO identities. MEDIUM confidence on governance structure - searches did not surface a UA-Tuscaloosa university-wide AI committee with public membership (UAB has one; UA does not appear to). LOW confidence on: exact user counts for free Copilot (not published), any Claude for Education or ChatGPT Edu deals (none found but absence of evidence), and whether President Mohler has made a formal AI statement since taking office July 2025 (none surfaced). No AI-related litigation or controversy identified.
